kicker: PT LEVEL I // GENERAL APPLICATIONS OF EACH METHOD

beats.narration: Knowing how each method works is half the job. Knowing where each one belongs is the other half — and that is a system decision.

payload.title: WHERE EACH METHOD BELONGS
payload.sub: Match the method to the job, not the habit.

beats.narration: Five inputs drive the call: what the spec demands, what the part is, its surface, how many are coming, and where the work happens.
That sorts the methods into territory. A rinses volume and rough skins. B and D protect tight cracks on machined parts. C goes anywhere, by hand.
Then place closes the loop. Fluorescent needs a dark booth and strong U-V. On open steel at noon, field work defaults to visible dye.
So run the checklist in order: spec, part, surface, quantity, place. Answer honestly and the method usually picks itself — then confirm against the procedure.

payload.sub: The method picks itself — then confirm against the written procedure.
